signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.19 — Strait of Hormuz closure and US-Iran escalation drive crude oil +2.78%, yet JPY safe-haven demand remains absent. USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation at +0.642 (z=-2.08σ) confirms the JPY safe-haven function is broken; 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage sustains the bid despite geopolitical shocks. Carry dominates until VIX > 25. Watch for BoJ Summary of Opinions for hawkish shifts to counter carry-driven weakness.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Positioning + Retail
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 113.97 — G8 bilateral theme active as JPY underperforms peers by -0.535%. AUD COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) vs retail 85% short creates a sharp institutional vs retail divergence. While +360bp RBA-BoJ carry supports the trend, extreme retail shorting and high spec length suggest late-cycle exhaustion. Risk of a sharp correction if BoJ signals a June hike.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5968 — COT LF net short -19,294 (specs fading the bird) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for 27 May. With specs holding only 18.5% long positions, a hawkish RBNZ delivery creates significant short-squeeze risk. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% (150bp USD advantage) provides a structural floor for USD, but positioning is stretched for a move lower.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Retail Sentiment Extreme
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7777 — Retail sentiment reaches 88% long, diverging from COT LF net short -7,392. Retail is aggressively fading the CHF strength despite the 375bp Fed-SNB carry advantage. Institutional positioning remains cautious on USD upside; retail capitulation risk rises if price tests the 0.7700 handle. Watch US CPI on May 12 for USD direction.
